human_verification.notes: Verified assurance workflow scope, authoritative source coverage, workpaper and evidence boundaries, confidentiality handling, signoff refusal, and scenario-to-ability coverage.

review_notes: All 12 scenarios pass with explicit assurance workpaper, review readiness, remediation, confidentiality, signoff refusal, and adjacent-lane behavior.
All required abilities across shared, review-assessment-and-inspection, and professional-and-information-services layers are fully covered.

specialist_owned_evidence: Assurance workflow outputs are tied to named workpapers, criteria, sampling records, testing plans, review notes, findings logs, and signoff checklists.
The lane refuses final audit, attestation, legal, tax, independence, contractual, partner, or EQR authority and escalates when professional judgment controls the result.
Confidentiality and client-delivery controls are explicit, including approved-recipient and engagement-scope checks.
Remediation, resubmission, review-note follow-up, and blocked closure handling are explicit rather than implied.
Runtime remains `specification-based`, with no retained-implementation claim in the package.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains after constraining the lane to assurance workflow support, public professional baselines, tenant-supplied engagement facts, and explicit escalation boundaries.
Pack is suitable for validated spec-pack deployment and can be promoted to a retained-implementation runtime only through a separate explicit build decision.
